caption: Jatayu battles Ravana to save Sita
scene: Ravana in his flying chariot carrying off Sita, while the noble vulture-king Jatayu attacks him in the sky with outstretched wings and talons, trying to rescue mother Sita. Sita reaches out in distress. Dramatic, heroic sacrifice.
tool: PLACEHOLDER — record the actual generator when the real image is added
prompt: Traditional Tanjore (Thanjavur) style devotional painting, wide landscape 16:9: the fearsome demon-king Ravana — shown as a single crowned head and two arms, a mighty richly-adorned rakshasa emperor with a dark complexion and fierce moustached face — abducting Sita, carrying her away through the sky in the Pushpaka Vimana, a celestial self-flying golden palace-chariot (an ornate flying aerial pavilion with a swan/peacock-shaped prow, NO wheels and NO horses — it flies by itself through the air on clouds). The noble aged vulture-king Jatayu — an old, majestic divine bird with WHITE plumage and vast outstretched white wings — attacks Ravana heroically in mid-air to rescue Sita, talons and beak striking; Sita in simple forest attire reaches toward Jatayu in distress. Below is only dense wild forest, hills and a river — NO cities, NO palaces, NO buildings (the Dandaka wilderness). Sky-and-cloud setting. Rich jewel-tone colors, gold-leaf gilded ornamentation and halos, ornate arched temple border, dramatic composition, classical South Indian temple art, no text, no lettering. NOTE: Rama and Lakshmana are NOT present at the abduction.
